title: Elymus zhui
taxonomy: Elymus zhui is classified as a species of grass in the family Poaceae. It belongs to the genus Elymus, which includes several species of grasses native to various parts of the world. Within the genus Elymus, Elymus zhui is further classified as part of the section Stipophorum. This classification helps to identify and distinguish Elymus zhui from other similar species within the same genus. Further research may be necessary to confirm the exact classification and relationships within the family Poaceae.

intro: Elymus zhui, also known as Tu Fu Ling, is a type of perennial grass that belongs to the Poaceae family. It is valued for its medicinal properties, particularly in traditional Chinese medicine, where it is used to treat a range of health issues, including fever, rheumatism, and digestive problems. The bioactive constituents of Elymus zhui include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and polysaccharides, which are responsible for its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant activities. In herbal medicine, Elymus zhui is commonly prepared as a decoction or a tincture, often used in combination with other herbs to enhance its therapeutic effects.
